info.name: Bee Flow
info.summary: AI assistant for your Nextcloud — chat with Files, Mail, Calendar, Deck, Notes, Tasks, Contacts and Talk.
info.description:
> ✅ **Install in one click from the Nextcloud App Store** — no setup needed on most Nextclouds, including Nextcloud All-in-One and other managed/hosted setups. A few self-managed servers first need AppAPI's deploy daemon enabled; the short [install guide](https://docs.beeflow.ai/getting-started/nextcloud) shows how.
**Bee Flow turns your Nextcloud into an AI workspace.**
Click the bee icon in your top bar and chat with an assistant that already knows your files, mail, calendar and team. Ask it to summarise a long PDF, draft a reply to an email, find that document from last quarter, or schedule a meeting — Bee Flow does the work in the apps you already use.
## What you get
- **One-click install, zero configuration.** The install connects your Nextcloud to **Bee Flow Cloud** — our hosted service, which runs on **Scaleway** with all servers located in **the Netherlands (EU)**. Sign in with your existing Nextcloud account — no second password, no app-passwords, no API keys to paste anywhere. - **An AI agent with context.** Bee Flow can read and act on **Files, Mail, Calendar, Contacts, Deck, Notes, Tasks, Talk, Activity** and **Notifications** — only on the items you explicitly point it at. - **Built-in privacy shield.** Detects emails, phone numbers, IBANs, BSNs and other sensitive data in your prompts and replaces them with placeholders before anything reaches the language model. The original values are restored in the reply, only on your screen. - **Multi-user from day one.** Mirrors your Nextcloud users and groups automatically. Admins decide which groups get which Bee Flow integrations — disable Talk for interns, enable everything for the leadership team. - **Self-service for organisation admins.** A 4-step onboarding wizard walks the admin through user-sync mode, default permissions and privacy settings the first time they open Bee Flow. Re-configure anytime under Settings → Organisation.
## How it works
This Nextcloud app is a thin connector. It runs as an ExApp container next to your Nextcloud and connects the embedded Bee Flow web client (an open-source React SPA) to **Bee Flow Cloud**, our hosted service running on **Scaleway** with all servers located in **the Netherlands (EU)**. Browser → Nextcloud signed proxy → connector → Bee Flow Cloud. Your Nextcloud session is the only credential.
**Prefer to keep everything on your own infrastructure?** You can also run Bee Flow fully self-hosted on your own servers — the connector, the SPA and the Bee Flow server are all open source ([Bee-Flow on GitHub](https://github.com/Bee-Flow)), and Bee Flow Cloud runs the very same code. Follow the [self-hosting guide](https://docs.beeflow.ai/self-hosting) to deploy your own Bee Flow server, then point this connector at it instead of Bee Flow Cloud (Settings → Bee Flow server URL).
## Data flow disclosure
When you act on a Nextcloud item, the connector sends the item to the Bee Flow service over TLS. Specifically:
- **Always** — your Nextcloud user ID, email, display name and group memberships (used to identify your tenant and apply group-based permissions). - **On demand** — the contents of items you explicitly point the assistant at (e.g. an email you ask it to summarise, a file you ask it to read).
Bee Flow **does not** bulk-export file contents, mail bodies, calendars or contacts. The connector forwards individual requests; nothing is copied, mirrored or indexed by default.
Bee Flow Cloud is hosted on **Scaleway**, with all servers located in **the Netherlands (EU)** — your data is processed and stored within the EU.
Full privacy policy: <https://beeflow.nl/privacy>.
## Getting started
1. Install this app from the Nextcloud App Store. 2. Open Bee Flow from the top bar. 3. The first time the organisation admin opens it, the onboarding wizard runs automatically — pick your user-sync mode and privacy settings. 4. Other users see a "Setup in progress" screen until the admin finishes — then the chat opens for everyone in the chosen sync scope.
## Support
Questions, or want a hand getting set up? Email us at **info@beeflow.nl**. We support Bee Flow Cloud and can also help you get Bee Flow running on a self-managed or self-hosted Nextcloud.
info.author: Bee Flow B.V.

variable.name: BEEFLOW_TENANT_KEY
variable.description: Leave at "auto" to automatically create a NEW Bee Flow organisation the first time the connector starts, at whichever server is set in "Bee Flow server" below (Bee Flow Cloud by default). Override with an existing tenant key only to connect this Nextcloud to an organisation that already exists (e.g. an enterprise reinstall). To join an existing organisation without a key, use a pairing code below.

variable.name: BEEFLOW_API_BASE_URL
variable.display-name: Bee Flow server — Cloud (default) or self-hosted
variable.description: Where the connector sends API calls. The default, https://server.beeflow.nl, is Bee Flow Cloud: installing with this default creates a new Bee Flow organisation hosted by Bee Flow B.V. (EU) and your data is stored there. To self-host instead and keep data on your own infrastructure, run your own Bee Flow server (the Community tier is free) and enter its URL here. See https://beeflow.nl.

variable.name: BEEFLOW_NC_PUBLIC_URL
variable.description: Public URL where Bee Flow Cloud can call this Nextcloud back during bootstrap and for user-sync. Leave blank if your Nextcloud is already publicly reachable at its main URL — the common case. Set this only if your Nextcloud is behind NAT or only reachable via a tunnel/reverse-proxy. The picker in /setup also writes here at runtime.

variable.name: BEEFLOW_PAIRING_CODE
variable.description: One-shot 8-char code generated by the org admin in an existing Bee Flow organisation (Settings → Organisation → Nextcloud Sync → "Pair a new Nextcloud"). Setting this before first boot binds this Nextcloud to that existing organisation instead of auto-creating a new one at Bee Flow Cloud. The code is consumed once during bootstrap and ignored afterwards — safe to leave set, but you can also clear it after a successful bind.

variable.name: BEEFLOW_NC_CA_CERT
variable.description: Leave blank in almost all cases. The connector automatically trusts a self-signed or internal-CA Nextcloud certificate on first start (it only does this when the certificate does not already verify, so valid public/Let's Encrypt certificates are unaffected). Set this only if you prefer to pin an explicit trust anchor: paste the PEM certificate (or chain) of your Nextcloud's CA. Has no effect when your Nextcloud already uses a publicly-trusted certificate.

variable.name: BEEFLOW_NC_TLS_PIN
variable.description: "auto" (default) lets the connector trust your Nextcloud's certificate on first start when it is self-signed or signed by an internal CA, so the app can complete setup on local/on-prem installs. This never weakens a valid public certificate. Set to "off" in security-strict environments to disable automatic trust — then only a publicly-trusted certificate or an explicit "Nextcloud CA certificate" above will be accepted.
